message: Jake
Yuriko
Gloria
When I returned to the office, lost in thought, she was already gone, and
a rare guest had come in her place.
Ah
It's been a while; sorry to drop by.
? What have you been up to? We haven't seen each other in so long.
I was thinking of going back to Hong Kong.
I met Meryl today. She asked me to deliver a message.
Meryl has a message? For me?
Yeah. She said,
Don't worry about me; being arrested was my own choice, so this is fine.
And,
Meryl isn't the one who killed Guy and Kristy, right?
Well,
I can’t say. I just came to deliver her message.
Saying this,
she smiled sadly.
Oh, right.
Hey,
isn't this bear brooch possibly Cindy's?
Oh, this?
Where was it?
Cindy had it, um,
It seems like it’s better not to delve too deeply. Can I take this home?
Oh, sure. It wouldn't be of any use to me anyway.
I'm so glad. This was something Cindy got from Jennifer,
and she treasured it.
Jennifer.
That's right, Cindy and Jennifer were close, right?
Uh, yeah. She dropped by the hotel, but I wonder if she’s already gone home.
Do you know where Jennifer's house is?
I think she’s probably at the Cape Cod house she bought recently.
I came
to drag the address out of him, then headed to Cape Cod to meet Jennifer.
−　The next day　−
Good morning, Boss.
Good morning.
Yesterday was a bit shocking. Just remembering it still makes my stomach churn.
Are you okay?
Yeah, somehow. I can’t let something like this get me down if I want to be a great detective.
Welcome back, Boss!
Thanks for your hard work.
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
Surroundings
About Jennifer
About the Decoy House
About the Serial Murder Case
about it
About Jennifer
About the Decoy House
About the Serial Murder Case
To another place
Save
Exit the game.
A familiar room, my desk that I know down to the last scratch.
But strangely, I feel no attachment to those things at all.
I could throw this room away and go anywhere at any time.
Even though it should be a familiar room, it feels uncomfortable, like someone else's house. Why is that?
I can't believe you're leaving.
Looks away from me and stares at the monitor with a stiff expression.
Sitting in front of the monitor.
I can't imagine you not being here.
I can't even picture such a thing.
It’s hard to accept that you're leaving.
As if already knowing what's about to happen, that expression looks lonely.
Nervously shifting their gaze, they won’t meet my eyes. It’s somehow different from the way they were always sincere.
There's something off about it.
Hey Boss, please cheer up.
I’m doing fine.
But...
Are you really okay?
Thank you for taking care of me all this time. Really, I'm grateful.
It’s something I’ve already decided. So, I’m sorry, but please let me quit.
Is it really something you can’t change?
I’m sorry.
I enjoyed working here; it will be a good memory.
I can’t imagine this office without you. I still want you to be working here.
Thank you. However, I've actually been thinking about this for quite some time.
Is it because you can't do what you want in this office?
No, I was happy to be able to use my time freely here. It's not that.
I’m sorry, Boss.
I'm really sorry, Boss.
I’m quitting today. I can no longer work under you.
It's fine; I don’t worry about the past.
If you’re okay with it, I’m not.
I'm sorry, Boss! Actually, I was helping Jennifer.
Huh!?
Jennifer asked me to help her because she has feelings for you, so I cooperated at first.
But surely...
I'm sure they asked me all sorts of questions to gather information.
Really?
So,
the matter of the Decoy House is my fault. It's because I talked that she went to cover up the evidence.
Boss.
I.
No, it's nothing.
I'm sorry, Boss.
Please meet with Jennifer and check. I'm worried too.
I have no idea how to bring it up.
Not a clue.
Y-yeah, that's true.
It would be great if Jennifer came to us.
I don't know where Jennifer went after she checked out of the hotel.
Was Jennifer already gone?
I see.
Boss.
Is she really the culprit?
I'm sorry, Boss.
They said there was nothing left at the Decoy House. Was that coffin just a hallucination?
A hallucination, really?
We all saw it together, right? But I had no idea such terrible things were happening.
It was definitely there.
You saw it too, right, Boss?
The problem is that the title for the Decoy House might be fake. A culprit with a grudge against her could have done it in her name.
Why would anyone go through such trouble?
I don't think so.
I might have stumbled upon something huge.
I'm sorry, Boss.
Do you think Jennifer is the culprit?
In the current situation, I have no choice but to think that way.
Yeah, that's right after all.
Jennifer was the killer.
So, in the end, it means that the serial murder case was her doing, right?
We still don't know that.
I feel like I already understand enough.
What should I do?
I regret telling Jennifer everything, or at least that's the front I’m putting on.
But this doesn’t seem like something that just occurred to me out of nowhere.
We've shared a lot of time together up until now. I never imagined a day like this would come.
But what if...
she's not in this office anymore?
If possible, I want her to stay in the office as is.
Right around the time I saw that in the Decoy House, she was clearing out the hotel room. That must have been Jennifer's.
If Jennifer has fled, where might she go?
To leave the hotel without a word to me...
Is it really all Jennifer's doing?
We shouldn't rush to conclusions. Until we hear the truth directly from Jennifer, we can't assume it's her doing.
That room in the Decoy House belonged to Jennifer. In other words, the gruesome stuff in that room...
was hers.
also hers
Anyway, I need to meet with Jennifer.
Who carried the coffin out of that room?
How did they know we had stepped into that room?
Even if the coffin is gone, nothing changes. The bloodstains left in that room tell the story of the horrific events that took place there.
I can’t believe that she was the owner of that room. I want to deny it, but it’s an undeniable fact. However, that doesn’t necessarily mean she is the killer.
What I saw over there was definitely not a dream. I wish it were a dream, though.
But it's all reality.
We discovered a coffin and an abominable creation in the Decoy House, and when the police barged into the room, the coffin disappeared, and Jennifer also vanished.
I can't believe there's absolutely no connection to these events. Could the culprit really be Jennifer?
I have to meet Jennifer no matter what. Until I hear the truth from her, I can't draw any conclusions.
It's certainly indisputable that the Decoy House belongs to Jennifer, but that doesn't mean she is the culprit.
It's also possible that someone tried to frame Jennifer by committing murder in her room.
In any case, let's not rush to conclusions.
※※※ It’s a bug ※※※
